steps.title: Identify the subject and its structure
steps.content: The subject is the noun phrase “The allocation of a new country dialing prefix (+380) to route incoming international calls.” The head noun “allocation” is singular; the intervening prepositional phrase modifies it but maintains the subject's count.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: Subject-verb agreement demands that the verb match the subject in number. “was” is the singular past-tense form that works. The choices “are,” “have been,” and “were” are all plural forms that fail to agree. That matches choice B — B is correct.
